article_title: Supreme Court Denies Apple Stay in Epic Contempt Case, Setting Up June 25 Decision on Cert Petition
summary: All claims and quotes verified across six sources; allowlist warnings are administrative only — appleinsider.com and perkinscoie.com are credible tech news and law firm analysis

content_preview.title: Supreme Court Denies Apple Stay in Epic Contempt Case, Setting Up June 25 Decision on Cert Petition
content_preview.summary: Justice Kagan rejected Apple's emergency stay request in under an hour on May 6, and Apple has now formally petitioned the Supreme Court to review the contempt finding and injunction scope.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Two new developments have accelerated the App Store legal saga that The Machine Herald [previously covered in April](/article/2026-04/12-apple-petitions-supreme-court-a-second-time-over-app-store-fees-as-epic-games-contempt-battle-enters-sixth-year): on May 6, Justice Elena Kagan denied Apple's emergency request to pause a civil contempt order, ruling in under an hour and without referring the matter to the full court; and on May 21, Apple formally filed a certiorari petition asking...

recommendations: Consider adding appleinsider.com and perkinscoie.com to config/source_allowlist.txt as credible Apple-focused tech coverage and law firm legal analysis

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 836-word News article covering two distinct legal developments in the Apple/Epic saga: the May 6 stay denial and the May 21 cert petition. Good narrative organization. The cross-reference to the April article is appropriate. The detailed explanation of Apple's two petition questions and the contempt findings background is particularly useful.
editor_notes.source_verification: All six sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (9to5Mac May 21): confirmed June 25 conference date, 'Hail Mary' Epic quote, Munoz name/title. source-1.html.gz (MacRumors May 21): confirmed June 25 conference, Justice Kagan stay denial, 'spirit-based inquiry is antithetical' Apple petition language verbatim, Natalie Munoz Director of Corporate Communications title, 'one last Hail Mary' quote verbatim, worldwide developers injunction scope argument, January 2024 Supreme Court prior rejection, April 2025 contempt finding. source-2.html.gz (SCOTUSblog): confirmed Kagan denial, 'less than an hour' after Epic opposition distributed, 'erroneous and prejudicial contempt label' Apple quote verbatim, 'Apple's willful contempt has successfully delayed the restoration of competition by more than two years' Epic quote, 'Great news – the Supreme Court denied Apple's delay tactics' Epic post-denial quote. source-3.html.gz (9to5Mac May 6): confirmed Kagan stay denial, 27% commission. source-4.html.gz (AppleInsider): confirmed Kagan denial, 'erroneous and prejudicial contempt label' Apple quote, April 2025 contempt finding, zero commission since then. source-5.html.gz (Perkins Coie): confirmed 'link entitlement program', 27% commission, 'seven days of the click-through', perjury finding, 2023 decision predating commissioned report, U.S. Attorney referral for potential criminal contempt. No discrepancies found.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All direct quotes verified verbatim in sources. Apple quotes confirmed in SCOTUSblog and MacRumors. Epic quotes confirmed in SCOTUSblog and MacRumors. Munoz/title confirmed in MacRumors. Contempt details (27%, 7-day window, perjury, U.S. Attorney) confirmed in Perkins Coie. Legal procedural facts are accurate.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and appropriately presenting both Apple's and Epic's positions. Legal framing is accurate without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: This article updates the April 12 coverage with distinct new developments (May 6 stay denial + May 21 cert petition). Not a duplicate. Cross-reference to the April article is appropriate.
recommendations: Consider adding appleinsider.com and perkinscoie.com to config/source_allowlist.txt
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong legal/tech policy news article covering two discrete procedural developments. All quotes verified verbatim. Cross-referencing prior coverage appropriately. Ready for publication.
